description_long: This object is a tall, upright stone marker with a rounded top and ornate relief carvings on its front face. The upper section features a prominent skull and crossbones motif framed by decorative scrollwork, suggesting a historical or memorial function. The stone surface is weathered, with visible signs of age such as minor chipping and moss growth along the base. The back of the object is plain, with only faint surface texture and no decorative elements. The sides are straight and relatively unadorned, showing the thickness and solid construction of the stone. The overall appearance is somber and robust, typical of guard stones or boundary markers found in older urban or historical settings.
description: A tall, weathered stone guard stone with ornate skull and crossbones relief on the front and moss at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing detailed skull and crossbones relief with decorative scrollwork and a weathered stone surface.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the stone and the edge of the relief, with some moss at the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, undecorated stone surface with faint texture and moss along the bottom edge.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view highlighting the depth of the relief carving and the solid, unadorned stone back.
attribute_location: Typically found at the edge of historical urban streets, near the corners of old buildings, or at the entrance to courtyards and driveways, often serving as a protective marker.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be installed upright, partially embedded in the ground for stability, with the decorated face oriented toward the street or pathway.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible weathering and erosion on the upper and front surfaces, minor chipping along the edges, and moss or lichen growth at the base due to prolonged exposure to the elements and possible contact with vehicles or foot traffic.
attribute_historical_markings: Ornate relief carving of a skull and crossbones with scrollwork on the front face; no visible inscriptions or text.
